segments.text: Almost a year has passed since I came down here at your headmaster's kind invitation.
In order to chair myself and share the hearts of a few of my friends, I see some of our own songs.
The ten months that have passed have seen very terrible catastrophic events in the world,
ups and downs, misfortunes, but can anyone sitting here this afternoon, this October afternoon,
not feel deeply thankful for what has happened in the time that has passed,
and for the very great improvement in the position of our country and of our home.
Why, when I was here last time, we were quite alone, desperately alone,
alone. And we had been so from five or six months, we were poorly armed, we were not poorly
armed today. But then we were very poorly armed. We had the unmeasured minutes of the enemy
and their air attack still beating upon us. And you we ourselves had had experience of
this attack. And I expect you are beginning to feel impatient that there has been this
long love, with nothing particular turning up.
You cannot tell from appearances how things should go.
Sometimes imagination makes things hard for worse than they are, yet without imagination
not much can be done.
Though it's people who are imaginative, see many more dangers than perhaps resist.
Certainly many more than will happen, but then they must also pray to be given that extra
courage to carry this far reaching imagination. But for everyone, surely, what we've
gone through in this period, I'm addressing myself to the school, surely in this period
of ten months, this is the lesson. Never give in. Never give in. Never, never, never. In
nothing, great or small, large or petty, never give in except the convictions of honor
and good sense. Never yield to force, never yield to the appearance of overwhelming might
of the enemy. We stood all alone a year ago, and to many countries it seemed that are
a conquest out. We were finished. All this tradition of ours, our school history, our songs,
this part of the history of our country, all were gone and finished and liquidated. There
different is removed today. Britain, other nations sought a drawn
astonishment across our slate. But instead our country stood in the gap. There was no
finishing, no thought of giving in. And by what seemed almost
too medical to those outside the islands, that we ourselves never doubted it. We now
find ourselves in a position where I say we can be sure that we have only to
first year we are to conquer. You sang here a verse of a school song, you sang that
extra verse written in my honor because I was very greatly complimented by it and we should
have repeated today. But there is one word in it I want to alter. I wanted to do so last year,
but I did not venture to. It is a line, not less we praise in darker days.
I obtained the headmaster to mission to alter DACA to stoner, not less we pray in stoner
days.
Do not let the streak of DACA days, let the streak rather stoner days, these are not DAC days,
these are great days, the greatest our country has ever lived, and we would all thank God
that we have been allowed each of us according to our station to play a part in making these days memorable in the history of our race.
